project.name: BLOXIAFramework
project.purpose: Foundation library for BLOXIA, a Roblox game-development IDE workflow that guides AI-generated code toward modular Bloxia services/controllers/components/systems, Jecs simulation and optimized Lync networking.

shared.role: Public library surface shared by server and client.

framework.role: BLOXIA-owned lifecycle, module registration, component binding, system loops and dependency facade.
framework.rule: Gameplay modules should use Bloxia.Service, Bloxia.Controller, Bloxia.Component and Bloxia.System. Knit is not part of the runtime.

ecs.role: Jecs facade, world registry and ordered ECS component ID registry.
ecs.rule: Use Bloxia.ECS from systems. Declare ECS component IDs under src/shared/Bloxia/ECS/Components and register them in ECS/Registry.luau.

network.role: Single source of truth for Lync packets, queries, groups and codecs.
network.rule: Every Lync packet, query and group must be defined before Bloxia.Net.start(). Do not define network contracts inside services/controllers.

server.role: Bloxia service layer. Services own gameplay, persistence and server authority.

client.role: Bloxia controller layer. Controllers own local input, presentation and UI orchestration.

metadata.strategy: Use this root metadata.json as the primary AI-readable manifest. Add module-level metadata only for large or ambiguous subsystems.
metadata.reason: Per-file meta files create maintenance noise and may interfere with Rojo-synced paths if placed inside src.

libraryRegistry.role: Defines how BLOXIA searches, selects and installs reusable pluggable libraries from a cloud registry.

cli.role: Local validation and module generation shortcuts for the BLOXIA IDE and developers.

config.role: Machine-readable project configuration for paths, registry settings and template defaults.

cloudRegistry.purpose: Store reviewed pluggable BLOXIA libraries so the IDE can reuse existing systems before generating new code.
lookupFlow: Parse user request into feature intent.
Search remote libraries by tags, summary and public API.
Filter candidates by runtime compatibility, dependencies and public API fit.
Ask the user to choose when multiple candidates match or configuration is required.
Install selected files into BLOXIA folders and register NetSchemas before network start.
Validate Rojo metadata, JSON and project conventions after install.
compatibilityRules: Reject gameplay remotes outside Lync NetSchemas.
Reject components outside src/server/Components or src/client/Components.
Reject systems outside src/server/Systems or src/client/Systems.
Reject ECS component IDs outside src/shared/Bloxia/ECS/Components.
Require confirmation for ProfileStore data migrations.
Require compatible BLOXIA runtime and dependency versions.

Lync.role: Authoritative networking layer using codecs, packets, queries, groups and buffer serialization.

QuickZone.role: Spatial query and zone tracking subsystem.

Fusion.role: Reactive UI construction on the client.

jecs.role: Data-oriented entity-component-system simulation used through Bloxia.ECS and Bloxia.System.

ProfileStore.role: Player persistence and session locking. PlayerDataService uses Mock in Studio by default.

decisionMap.Service: Server authority, public feature APIs, persistence, economy and anti-cheat.
decisionMap.Controller: Client input, camera, UI orchestration, prediction and presentation.
decisionMap.Component: Behavior attached to tagged Roblox instances.
decisionMap.System: Jecs simulation over many entities or data rows.

dependencyRules: Services may depend only on server Services.
Controllers may depend only on client Controllers.
Server Components and Systems may depend only on server Services.
Client Components and Systems may depend only on client Controllers.
Use Bloxia.Net instead of DependsOn for server/client communication.
rules: Use the decisionMap before creating any file.
Create server gameplay as Bloxia services under src/server/Services.
Create client behavior as Bloxia controllers under src/client/Controllers.
Create tag-based instance behavior as Bloxia components under src/server/Components or src/client/Components.
Create data-oriented simulation as Bloxia systems under src/server/Systems or src/client/Systems.
Create ECS component IDs under src/shared/Bloxia/ECS/Components and register them in src/shared/Bloxia/ECS/Registry.luau.
Create UI through Fusion modules under src/client/UI.
Create persistence through server-only modules and PlayerDataService.
Create all networking contracts under src/shared/Bloxia/Net/Schemas and register them in Registry.luau.
Never create ad hoc RemoteEvent or RemoteFunction instances for gameplay features.
Never require Packages.jecs directly from feature code; use Bloxia.ECS.
Never define Lync packets/queries/groups after Bloxia.Net.start().
Never create gameplay networking inside services, controllers, components or systems.
Prefer explicit Lync codecs over Lync.auto for production gameplay payloads.
Use Lync groups for audience targeting instead of sending repeated individual packets when possible.
Keep Services authoritative. Controllers should request, display or predict, not own server truth.

bloxia.config.json.instruction: Used by BLOXIA tooling and IDE integrations. Roblox runtime code does not read this file directly.

.bloxia/templates.instruction: Agents should prefer these templates when creating new Services, Controllers, Components, NetSchemas, UI components or data modules.

src/shared/Bloxia/Framework/init.luau.instruction: Public lifecycle API for Bloxia services, controllers and components. This is a native BLOXIA runtime.

src/shared/Bloxia/ECS/Registry.luau.instruction: Add new ECS component modules to orderedComponents in stable order. Changing order changes Jecs component IDs.

src/shared/Bloxia/Net/Registry.luau.instruction: Add new schema modules to orderedSchemas in stable order. Changing order changes Lync IDs.

src/shared/Bloxia/Net/Schemas/PlayerNet..instruction: Owns player data packets and queries. Keep payloads small and explicit.

src/shared/Bloxia/Net/Schemas/ZoneNet.lu.instruction: Owns zone packets and Lync groups. New zone groups must be defined here before network start.
